title: viftyctl capabilities --json report
description: Machine-readable capabilities report emitted by viftyctl capabilities --json. Agents should use this payload to discover supported commands, workloads, policy data, and exit-code meanings instead of hardcoding them; policy limits are trusted for cooling only when policyStatusAvailable is true, and cooling may be requested only when policy.enabled is true.

policyStatusAvailable.description: True only when policy limits were read from daemon agent-control status. Agents must not trust policy.* duration/RPM limits for cooling when this field is false or absent.

supportsForceRetry.description: True when viftyctl run --force supports a supervised one-time wait for retryAfterSeconds and retry after a rate-limited prepare. Consumers reading legacy payloads without this field should treat force retry as unsupported.

enabled.description: True only when local agent cooling policy is enabled. Agents must refuse cooling when this field is false or absent.

runLifecycle.description: Machine-readable viftyctl run lifecycle guarantees that agents can depend on before deciding how much wrapper logic to add around a workload. Consumers reading legacy payloads without this object should treat every lifecycle guarantee as unsupported.

childCommandPreflightBeforeCooling.description: True when viftyctl run resolves and validates the child executable before preparing a cooling lease.

autoRestoreAfterChildExit.description: True when viftyctl run attempts to restore Auto after child launch failure, normal child exit, or handled signal exit.

structuredPreChildFailures.description: True when viftyctl run --json emits structured command-error reports before the child process starts.

cleanupStateReportedOnLaunchFailure.description: True when launch failures after a prepared lease report whether cleanup was attempted and whether Auto restore succeeded.

resolvedChildExecutableReported.description: True when successful completed viftyctl run --json reports include resolvedChildExecutable so agents can audit the absolute child executable selected before cooling was requested.

directControlLifecycle.description: Machine-readable direct prepare/restore lifecycle contract. Agents should prefer viftyctl run for single child workloads and use this object before deciding whether direct prepare/restore is safe for a supervised multi-step workflow.

prepareUsesIdempotencyKey.description: True when direct prepare/run requests carry idempotency keys so repeated prepare calls can return the existing active lease instead of reapplying cooling.

restoreAutoAcceptsIdempotencyKey.description: False when restore-auto rejects --idempotency-key instead of accepting a no-op scoped option.

restoreAutoScopedByIdempotencyKey.description: False when restore-auto restores the active supervised lease rather than selecting a lease by idempotency key.

preferRunForSingleChildWorkloads.description: True when agents should prefer viftyctl run for ordinary single child workloads so prepare, child execution, signal forwarding, and Auto restore remain one lifecycle.

metadataLimits.description: Machine-readable prepare/run metadata limits. Agents should use these values before constructing reason or idempotency-key strings instead of hardcoding local guesses.

maximumReasonLength.description: Maximum number of characters accepted for prepare/run reason metadata after trimming.

maximumIdempotencyKeyLength.description: Maximum number of characters accepted for prepare/run idempotency-key metadata after trimming.

wrapperResources.description: Relative source-tree and app-bundle locations for the audited guarded workload wrappers. Agents should use these paths and script names instead of hardcoding wrapper discovery. Paths are intentionally relative so evidence bundles do not leak user-specific install locations.

sourceDirectory.description: Source-checkout directory containing guarded-run.sh and workload shortcut wrappers.

bundleDirectory.description: App-bundle-relative directory containing the installed wrapper copies.

guardedRunScript.description: Primary guarded wrapper that performs read-only capabilities/readiness checks before delegating to viftyctl run --json.

workloadScripts.description: Audited convenience wrapper names available in source checkouts and installed app bundles.
